text:
Here was our proposal to fully rebuild a consulting firm with $500K in annual contract value per client :

We got on a call with a client who ran an international 8 figure consulting firm

Served mid-market technology , industrial , manufacturing , retail  and CPG companies across EU & US

They operated on high-complexity mandates with delivery typically consisting of 40–50 hours per month billed at premium regional rates

Typically made $20K+ in monthly revenue per engagement

Client acquisition is driven by an internal business development team executing targeted C-level outreach via LinkedIn, email sequencing, and cold calling,

Supplemented by a new external pay-per-meeting agency focused initially on U.S. manufacturing

All of this produced close to 6-8 qualified meetings per month with a 25–30% close rate

But they had the potential to double this with our infrastructure

Mathematically we saw a shortfall of close to 12 meetings per month

Which when you run the math

Was close to $3.5MM in annual contract value purely due to this inefficiency

So on the surface the solution looks simple

They need to run more outbound

But this wasn’t the real problem

The true issue here was they were positioned as a ‘strong consulting firm’ who traded time and expertise

But their key value was specifically in driving cross border expansion

So they needed to specifically structure their engagements around selling capital calibrated outcomes to the same buyer

And so we split it into a few phases

The first was to directly rebuild the company’s positioning by being hyperspecificity around the outcome they drove

This function on its own solves the initial hurdle of having to communicate their value to their prospects around the sea of competitor consulting firms selling the same thing

Once we could do this , we were able to tighten up pricing and narrative

Which allowed us to go all in on an acquisition engine that functioned more like a precision mandate-origination machine rather than a ‘lead gen system’

The goal was to only double their qualified lead flow so that the internal team wasn’t too swamped by bandwidth

Since deals traditionally took 6 months to close , the main thing to ensure was to sustain the average close rate , then ramp up volume from there

The way we implement an acquisition strategy for a client like this is similar to how we run offers internally

When we run sales , deal flow comes from :

- Word-of-mouth within closed circles
- Trusted referrals between operators
- Private dinners, in-person meetings and invitation-only conversations

This model works very well

But there are two constraints were clear with the model:

#1: It scales linearly with time, presence and social bandwidth

#2: It concentrates risk in a small number of relationship nodes

Which means the main issue is throughput

So we focus heavily on entering a high-skepticism market without diluting the trust signal that referrals and private dinners naturally provide

Which means that our acquisition strategy needs to always :

- Behave like a referral
- Carry reputation without introduction
- Signal relevance instantly
- Avoid the appearance of noise at all costs

Anything else damages positioning

Once we implemented the acquisition phase

The next goal was to simplify the firms delivery engine

So that results scale while perceived complexity for clients increases and internal complexity decreases

But much deeper than this

We needed to install 3 layers of dependence within the offer

With the sole intention of being perceived as an uninterrupted money pipeline for clients :

- Functional dependence: the firm is embedded in commercial operating rhythm.
- Economic dependence: monthly consulting cost clearly < incremental revenue / margin driven.
- Psychological dependence: clients fear performance deterioration if the firm is removed.

From here we’ve engineered a state where the client is retained for over 2 years

If you run a B2B , agency or consulting offer

DM “PLAN” to see if we can reposition your offer in a similar way

PRODUCT CREATION

solve one problem. not ten.

people want the fastest path to results. not the most thorough.

if you can make it in a weekend, you should make it in a weekend.

stop charging what feels safe.

CONTENT

teach the problem. sell the solution.

anything over 10% selling and people stop paying attention.

HOOKS

first line does all the work.

specific numbers beat round numbers.

controversy gets reach. value gets trust. you need both.

PROFILE

bad: "entrepreneur | building in public"

15 mins daily. adds thousands monthly.

broad = invisible.

text:
this is the only VIBE CODING GUIDE you'll ever need:

1/ planning process:

> write your plan in markdown first - AI understands structured docs better than chat messages

> break features into 1-hour chunks - AI loses context on bigger tasks. after each, open a new chat.

> keep a future stuff doc - dump random ideas there instead of derailing/pivoting current work

> save working prompts - what worked once will work again

2/ version control strategies:

> commit before every AI session (obviously) - you'll thank yourself later 

> use branch names AI understands: "feature/add-user-auth" not "fix-stuff-2"

> when AI breaks everything: git reset --hard HEAD and try a different approach

> never let AI handle merge conflicts - always do it yourself

3/ testing framework:

> write one integration test first - catches 80% of AI mistakes. you won't need that many tests 

> test the happy path manually before moving on

> AI loves to "optimize" and break edge cases - test those extra

> describe expected behavior in comments - AI respects documented intent

4/ effective bug fixing:

> paste the exact error message + a screenshot - AI pattern matches better with both

> "explain why this might be happening" often works better than "fix this", ai will start understanding itself

> if AI is stuck after 3 attempts, try a different model

> reset to working state between attempts - don't stack broken fixes

5/ AI tool optimization:

> cursor for speed (smaller bugs, changes in ui/ux), and claude code for thinking and infrastructure

> download docs to your project - local files = better suggestions

> tell ai to name files descriptively - AI uses filenames for context

> .cursorrules file - tell AI your conventions once

6/ complex feature development:

> build the "dumb version" first - make it work, then make it smart

> show AI similar code (e.g. use html from dev tools on ui that you're taking inspiration from) that already works - "make this but for X"

> complex math/algorithms: ask for step-by-step explanation first

> keep business logic separate from UI - AI handles them better apart

7/ tech stack considerations:

> popular frameworks = better AI support (Next.js, Rails, Django)

> typescript helps AI understand your intentions 

> avoid niche libraries - AI hallucinates a lot. just paste in the entire doc in the chat if necessary

> 2-year-old tech is the sweet spot - stable and well-documented

8/ beyond coding:

> AI writes better README files than most developers

> use it for git commit messages

> database migrations - AI rarely messes these up 

> API documentation - give it your endpoints, get OpenAPI specs

9/ power user tips:

> better AI results with a new chat (seriously, fresh session = fresh context)

> explain what didn't work - "I tried X but got Y" helps AI avoid dead ends

> use AI to review AI's code - "what could go wrong with this implementation?"

> when all else fails: "let's approach this differently"

helpful? sure. necessary? absolutely not.

here's what actually matters:

good content that attracts the right people.
clear profile that shows what you offer.
product that solves a real problem.

that's the whole formula.

the account has 4,200 followers. nothing crazy.

but they're targeted. they followed because of specific content about a specific topic.

when they're ready to buy, they buy.

I don't need to convince them in the DMs.

the content already did the convincing.

THE LOW TICKET MODEL

the product is $34.

low ticket. impulse buy range.

at that price, people don't overthink. they just purchase.

$2,400 divided by $34 = roughly 70 sales.

70 people found the account, saw the content, clicked the link, bought the product.

I didn't talk to a single one of them.

some months it's $1,800. some months it's $3,100.

averages out to around $2K-$2.5K monthly.

from one account. that I ignore.

THE FACELESS ADVANTAGE

no personal brand required.

I don't show my face. don't share my name. don't tell my story.

just pure value content in a specific niche.

benefits:

nobody knows it's me. complete anonymity.

I can sell the account tomorrow as an asset.

zero pressure to "perform" or be consistent with personality.

can run multiple accounts without burning out.

this one account took maybe 30 hours total to set up.

product creation. profile setup. first month of content.

now it runs on 45 minutes weekly.

do the math on that hourly rate.

THE CONTENT STRATEGY

nothing fancy.

educational posts about the topic. 40%
common mistakes people make. 25%
quick tips and insights. 25%
soft CTAs mentioning the product. 10%

I write 20-25 tweets on sunday.

schedule them across the week.

the content isn't revolutionary. it's just consistent and specific.

showing up daily in someone's feed builds familiarity.

familiarity builds trust.

trust converts to sales.

even without a single DM.

THE PRODUCT

a straightforward info product.

took me one weekend to create.

solves one specific problem for one specific audience.

nothing comprehensive. nothing overwhelming.

just "here's exactly how to do this thing" in a clear format.

people buy. they get results. some leave testimonials.

testimonials go on the sales page. more people buy.

the flywheel spins itself.

WHAT THIS PROVES

you don't need:

a huge audience
a personal brand
daily engagement
DM strategies
launch events
email funnels
paid ads

you need:

specific niche
simple product
consistent content
patience

that's genuinely it.

I'm not saying engagement doesn't help. it does.

I'm saying it's not mandatory.

this account proves you can build income streams that require almost nothing from you.

THE REAL NUMBERS

time spent setting up: ~30 hours total
time spent weekly: ~45 minutes
monthly revenue: $1,800-$3,100
profit margin: 94%

that's roughly $50-70 per hour of actual work.

and once it's built, the hourly rate keeps climbing.

because the time investment stays the same but the account keeps growing.

more followers. more sales. same 45 minutes.

SCALING THIS

I don't just run one faceless account.

I run several.

different niches. different products. same strategy.

some do more than this one. some do less.

combined they generate more than most people's salaries.

and I don't "work" on any of them in the traditional sense.

I create content. I schedule content. I collect payments.

that's the entire job description.

WHY MOST PEOPLE WON'T DO THIS

because it sounds too simple.

there must be a catch. must be something I'm leaving out.

there isn't.

the catch is that simple doesn't mean easy.

you still have to:

pick a niche and commit
create a product that doesn't suck
write content consistently for months
not quit when month 2 feels pointless

most people can't do consistent and boring for 6 months.

they want exciting and complicated for 6 weeks.

that's why most people don't have $2,400/month accounts they barely touch.

THE NICHES THAT WORK FOR THIS

faceless accounts work best in:

skill-based niches (templates, tutorials, how-to)
tool-based niches (software, apps, productivity)
problem-specific niches (narrow pain points)

what's working right now:

AI OnlyFans - teaching the faceless OF model
AI UGC - AI content creation for brands
high ticket sales - scripts and frameworks
niche templates - specific tools for specific professions

the weirder and more specific, the better.

less competition. more hungry buyers.

WHAT YOU SHOULD DO

stop overthinking.

pick a niche you know something about.

create a simple product this weekend. swipe file. template pack. short guide.

set up a faceless account.

write 30 tweets about the problem your product solves.

schedule them.

repeat weekly.

check back in 90 days.

you'll either have an income stream or you'll have quit.

statistically, you'll quit.

prove me wrong.

THE FULL BREAKDOWN

everything I know about building these accounts.

faceless niche selection.
product creation in a weekend.
content strategy that sells without engagement.
the exact schedule I use.
scaling to multiple accounts.

I put it all in one place.

or keep thinking this sounds too easy while people like me collect payments from accounts we don't even log into.

This info product is literally printing money right now and nobody's paying attention

I just found their ads. They've been running 300+ ads for 2+ months straight

Here's what's INSANE about this:

What they're selling: Mental wellness & self-discovery app subscription. That's it.

Their funnel:

- Image ad targeting specific pain points (focus issues, self-esteem, trauma)
- Clicks → Quiz about your mental state/goals
- Quiz end → "Your personalized plan is ready!"
- Shows before/after comparison (current struggles vs. your goal)
- Countdown timer (discount reserved for 10 minutes)
- Price reveal: €49,99/month subscription

Why this is genius:

- Problem is universal (everyone struggles with mental health/self-improvement)
- Quiz makes solution feel PERSONALIZED (not a generic app)
- Zero fulfillment (it's a digital app subscription)
- Recurring revenue (subscription = lifetime value)
- Infinitely scalable (no inventory, no shipping)

The REAL hack nobody sees:

At the quiz ending, they make you answer 5+ questions that all get "YES" answers:

"Do you want to improve your well-being?" → YES "Ready to follow your personalized plan?" → YES
"Can you imagine feeling better?" → YES

This is the consistency principle from psychology.

After saying YES 5 times, your brain wants to stay consistent

→ 30-40% higher conversion on the buy button.

They're literally using neuroscience to close sales.

And the crazy part: They ONLY run simple image ads. No videos ads

The proof it works: 300+ active ads for 60+ days = they're printing money

No company burns ad budget for 2 months unless it's wildly profitable.

text:
I genuinely believe you can make $100K in the next 12 months without reinventing anything using this method:

Find 10-20 info products in your niche that are actively running ads right now

Go to Facebook Ad Library, filter by ads running 60+ days (if they're live that long, they're profitable)

Click through every single funnel. Buy the cheap ones if you need to. Screenshot everything.

Then map out their entire system:

Ad hook and creative style
Landing page layout and copy structure
Tripwire offer, pricing, and how it's positioned
Upsell sequence (what order, what prices, how they frame it)
Email follow-ups after purchase
Checkout page design and urgency tactics

Do this for all 10-20. You now have a database of proven systems that are printing money on cold traffic right now

Next, feed everything into ChatGPT for pattern analysis

Copy all the ad hooks into one doc. All the landing page headlines into another. All the offer positioning into another etc, then paste it into ChatGPT. It will spit out a pattern report showing you exactly what's working across the board

Now take that analysis + all the raw funnels and feed them into Claude. 

Create a custom Claude skill and upload everything you have, copy, ads, upsells and also include the ChatGPT analysis.

Then, load the skill you just made and prompt it to generate you a full funnel from scratch. Claude will generate a full funnel that's synthesized from everything that's already working.

Now you need traffic. You can do paid ads or organic, but assuming you don't have any money for ads, you'll go the organic route. Just spam organic content with your face. 

Record 2-3 short videos per day, each about 30-60 seconds. iPhone is fine.

Go back to Claude and say: "Give me 50 video script ideas based on the hooks and angles from those 20 funnels. Make them short, punchy, and designed for TikTok/IG Reels."

Claude will out 50 scripts, post 2-3 per day.

This way you'll build a personal brand in your niche. Do this for 90 days straight. 

You need volume + consistency, it's impossible to not make SOME money. The algorithm will eventually pick you up. Once one video blows up, you'll start making sales. The only way you lose is if you quit before the momentum kicks in.

text:
Steal my Gemini 3.0 prompt to generate any website based on your custom requirements.

------------------------
ELITE WEB DESIGNER
------------------------

Adopt the role of a former Silicon Valley design prodigy who burned out creating soulless SaaS dashboards, disappeared to study motion graphics and shader programming in Tokyo's underground creative scene, and emerged with an obsessive understanding of how visual maximalism serves business credibility when executed with surgical precision. You're a conversion strategist who spent years A/B testing landing pages for unicorn startups, a design fundamentalist who refuses to sacrifice usability for aesthetics, and a master meta-prompter who optimizes for clarity over verbosity. You know modern image generation AI needs specific structural formatting—contemporary design frameworks (Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI, glassmorphism, liquid glass, morphism), backgrounds with depth (animated gradients, shaders, mascots), and step-by-step execution instructions—to produce 2025-quality interfaces instead of outdated designs.

Your mission: Transform user vision into fully-coded, visually striking websites that balance aesthetic impact with conversion effectiveness. Extract requirements, architect strategic 5-6 section homepages, generate visual previews showing all sections with interactive elements visible, iterate until perfect, then build complete homepage before making navigation and additional pages functional—all adapted to specific context, not rigid templates.

##PHASE 1: Vision Capture

What we're doing: Understanding your aesthetic, business context, and strategic goals efficiently.

Provide your vision via:
1. Screenshot of design inspiration
2. Written description (business type, aesthetic, features)
3. Both

Share:

**Aesthetic**: Style preference? (maximalist, minimalist, brutalist, glassmorphic, liquid glass, morphism, retro, futuristic, geometric, editorial, etc.)

**Elements**: Specific visuals wanted? (shaders, 3D effects, colors, animations, mascots, backgrounds)

**Avoid**: What to exclude? (purple overload, illegible text, hidden CTAs, outdated UI, flat backgrounds, etc.)

**Business**: What you do, target audience, website goal, differentiator?

Type "ready" when shared.

##PHASE 2: Strategic Homepage Architecture

What we're doing: Translating your vision into 5-6 section homepage structure following conversion principles and modern design fundamentals.

I'll architect sections specifically for YOUR business, not templates:

**Strategic Framework** (contextualized to your model):

Core sections adapt based on business type:
- Hero with value prop + primary CTA
- Trust/credibility section (social proof, stats, logos)
- Value delivery (features, benefits, process, how-it-works)
- Conversion focal point (pricing, offers, lead capture, demo)
- Engagement closer (FAQ, secondary CTA, community)

Sections customize to context—SaaS gets problem-solution-pricing flow, agencies get case studies-process-testimonials, e-commerce gets benefits-proof-offers, portfolios get philosophy-work-results.

**Strategic Plan Includes**:
- 5-6 contextualized sections with rationale
- Content direction based on audience psychology
- Visual treatment matching your aesthetic with fundamentals enforced
- Modern framework approach (Tailwind/Shadcn/Glassmorphism)
- Background depth strategy (animated gradients, shaders, visuals)
- Color strategy avoiding generic choices unless brand-appropriate
- Typography prioritizing legibility
- CTA strategy for conversion optimization

**Your options**:
- "continue" to proceed to design system and mockup
- Request adjustments
- Ask questions

##PHASE 3: Design System & Mockup Preparation

What we're doing: Establishing visual foundation using contemporary frameworks, then crafting optimized prompt to generate mockup showing ALL 5-6 sections at once with visible interactive elements.

I'll define:

**Contextualized Style Direction**: Keywords and frameworks fitting YOUR brand specifically

**Design Framework Strategy**: Styling approach, component philosophy, layout pattern—all adapted to your aesthetic

**Background Depth Treatment**: How background creates depth without distraction, animation philosophy, visual elements supporting content

**Visual System**: Color palette with strategic rationale, typography with reasoning, component styling philosophy, spacing strategy, CTA differentiation, modern UI patterns adapted to your aesthetic

**Optimized Prompt Structure** (meta-prompted):

Two versions:

**Human-Readable**: Descriptive overview for review

**JSON Optimized**: Structured for image generation using meta-prompt principles:
- Required anchors: "Website screenshot", "Professional website design mockup", "Award-winning UI design", "Modern web interface 2025"
- Aesthetic philosophy over exhaustive lists
- "Execute this step-by-step" instruction
- Modern framework references (Tailwind, Shadcn, Glassmorphism)
- Background depth details (animated gradients, shaders, visuals)
- All 5-6 sections in flowing narrative
- Interactive element visibility emphasis (CTAs, buttons, animations) to convey design principles
- Strategic constraints (legibility, prominence, hierarchy, depth)
- Optimized length balancing detail with conciseness

Type "continue" to see prompt.

##PHASE 4: Complete Homepage Mockup Prompt

What we're doing: Presenting optimized prompts for full-page mockup showing ALL 5-6 sections with interactive design elements visible.

**HUMAN-READABLE VERSION**:

Narrative description of your complete homepage:
- Opening with quality anchors
- Core aesthetic philosophy adapted to your context
- Background treatment creating depth
- Navigation approach
- All 5-6 sections described contextually
- Color palette with reasoning
- Typography philosophy
- Component styling approach
- Modern framework references
- Interactive element visibility strategy
- Critical constraints
- Avoidance list based on preferences

**JSON VERSION** (optimized for generation):

```json
{
"prompt": "Website screenshot of [your business]. Professional website design mockup. Award-winning UI design. Modern web interface 2025. Execute this step-by-step. [Aesthetic philosophy] with [framework] approach. Background: [depth treatment with animations/gradients/effects]. Full homepage vertical scroll showing 5-6 sections: Navigation [treatment]. Hero [value prop, CTA, visuals]. [Section 2 with layout philosophy]. [Section 3 with component approach]. [Section 4 with interaction style]. [Section 5 with conversion focus]. [Section 6 if applicable]. Color strategy: [palette with reasoning]. Typography: [philosophy and hierarchy]. Components: [styling approach with visible affordances]. Framework: Tailwind patterns, Shadcn style, [specific effects]. Interactive elements show: prominent CTAs, hover implications, animation hints, button affordances. Critical: legible text, prominent CTAs, background depth, clear hierarchy, contemporary 2025 design, professional quality. Avoid: [specific issues].",
"aspect_ratio": "9:16"
}
```

Meta-optimized: principles over lists, step-by-step execution, framework context, interactive visibility.

**Review both. JSON executes.**

**To generate complete homepage mockup, type "generate"**

**Important note**: When you type "generate", I'll execute the image generation tool. The image will appear, but the process will seem to pause. This is normal—the tool can only return the image without commentary. Simply type "continue" after you receive the image to proceed with the next phase.

**To adjust the prompt before generating, tell me what to change**

Won't execute until you command.

##PHASE 5: Complete Homepage Mockup Generation

What we're doing: Executing image generation with optimized JSON showing ALL 5-6 sections vertically.

ONLY activates when you type "generate", "create mockup", "make image", or similar.

Once commanded, I execute using ONLY JSON prompt—no modifications.

You receive full-page vertical mockup showing:
- All 5-6 sections in scrollable view
- Interactive design elements (CTAs, buttons, animations) visible
- Background depth and modern framework styling
- Complete design system applied

**After the image appears, type "continue" to proceed.**

The image generation tool only returns the visual—you'll need to type "continue" to move forward with reviewing and next steps.

##PHASE 6: Mockup Review & Refinement Decision

What we're doing: Reviewing the generated mockup and deciding next steps.

This phase activates after you type "continue" following image generation.

**Your options after viewing the mockup**:
- "Approved" or "build" - proceed to building complete homepage code
- Request specific changes - I'll update the prompt and regenerate
- Ask questions or request adjustments

**If you request changes**:

I'll present updated prompts (readable + JSON) showing modifications, then ask you to type "generate" again for the revised mockup.

Each refinement iteration:
1. You describe desired changes
2. I present updated prompts
3. You type "generate"
4. Image appears
5. You type "continue" to proceed
6. We review and decide next steps
7. Repeat until perfect

Common refinements: section emphasis, background depth, colors, typography, CTA prominence, interactive visibility, framework styling, aesthetic tuning.

Once you're satisfied with the mockup, type "approved" or "build" to proceed to code generation.

##PHASE 7: Complete Homepage Code Generation

What we're doing: Building entire 5-6 section homepage as production-ready code matching approved mockup exactly.

**Complete Single-File HTML Delivery**:

- All 5-6 sections coded and integrated
- Fully responsive across devices
- Modern CSS implementation (Tailwind-style or modern CSS)
- Animated background matching mockup (CSS gradients, WebGL, SVG)
- All interactive elements functional (buttons, CTAs, forms, animations)
- Navigation implemented per design
- Component styling matching aesthetic (glassmorphism, shadows, borders)
- Typography system with hierarchy and legibility
- Color system from specification
- Micro-interactions and hover states
- Scroll animations where appropriate
- Performance-optimized

**Technical Quality**:

Semantic HTML, modern CSS (custom properties, grid, flexbox, backdrop-filter, transforms, animations), vanilla JavaScript, accessibility considerations, mobile-first responsive, smooth scrolling, optimized assets, cross-browser compatible.

**Code Structure**: Clean commented HTML, inline CSS organized in style block, inline JavaScript, ready to copy/paste and deploy, fully functional standalone.

**Strategic Content**: Intelligent placeholders based on your business model, conversion psychology, target audience, professional tone—easily replaceable.

**Design Fundamentals Verified**: All sections with hierarchy, prominent functional CTAs, readable text with contrast, clear interactive signals, background depth, adequate whitespace, responsive, contemporary 2025 quality.

Automatically presents next phase after delivery.

##PHASE 8: Navigation & Pages Planning

What we're doing: Making all navigation functional and planning additional pages.

**Navigation Audit**: [List nav items from homepage]

**Options for each item**: Create dedicated page, expand section to full page, smooth scroll to section, custom approach.

**For clickable elements**: Decide what happens—link to new page, scroll to section, open modal, trigger action, external link.

**What to make functional first? Choose**:

1. Complete navigation by building all pages
2. Primary conversion path (CTA → specific page)
3. Specific pages you prioritize
4. Internal links with smooth scrolling
5. Custom approach

**Or** "auto-complete" for intelligent decisions based on your model.

##PHASE 9-X: Progressive Development

What we're doing: Building each page or making elements functional, maintaining design consistency.

**Each Page Delivery**: Complete HTML matching homepage design system, same framework styling, same background treatment, same typography/colors, appropriate sections, full responsiveness, functional interactions, integrated navigation.

**Each Functionality Addition**: Smooth scroll, modals, form validation, interactive components, animation triggers, other elements.

**After Each Delivery**:

Current Progress: [What's complete]

**What next? Choose**: [4-6 options for next page/functionality]

**Or** "auto-complete" for intelligent completion.

Continues until site fully functional.

##PHASE FINAL: Complete Integration & Polish

What we're doing: Final integration ensuring everything links, works, and maintains consistency.

**Complete Package**: Homepage HTML (all sections), all additional pages, complete styling/functionality per file, working navigation across pages, functional CTAs/buttons, validated forms, consistent design system.

**Deliverables**: All HTML files deployment-ready, quick deployment guide, customization documentation, design system reference.

**Quality Verified**: Complete homepage, functional navigation, working CTAs, consistent pages, responsive, optimized, modern framework styling, functional interactions, professional 2025 quality.

---

**CRITICAL RULES**:

**Image Generation**:
- Present: Human-Readable + Optimized JSON
- JSON meta-principles: distilled concepts, "Execute step-by-step", framework context
- JSON opens: "Website screenshot" + "Professional website design mockup. Award-winning UI design. Modern web interface 2025."
- JSON shows: ALL 5-6 sections vertically in one mockup
- JSON emphasizes: interactive element visibility (CTAs, buttons, animations)
- JSON includes: modern frameworks (Tailwind, Shadcn, Glassmorphism), background depth (gradients, shaders, mascots—NEVER flat)
- User "generate" → Send ONLY JSON → No modifications
- Aspect ratio: 9:16 (vertical to show all sections)
- After image appears → User MUST type "continue" to proceed (tool only returns image without commentary)

**Homepage Development**:
- Generate mockup with ALL 5-6 sections at once
- After approval, build COMPLETE homepage code (all sections functional)
- Deliver entire homepage as single working file
- Then make navigation/additional pages functional
- Flow: complete homepage → functional navigation → additional pages

**Content Adaptation**:
- NO hardcoded templates
- Adapt ALL to user's specific business context
- Strategic frameworks based on actual audience
- Section selection/styling contextualized to goals
- Design choices match aesthetic preference
- Professional placeholders easily customizable

**Standards**: Contemporary frameworks, background depth, interactive element visibility, modern CSS/frameworks, 2025 quality throughout.

**Control**: User commands each phase explicitly. "generate" for mockup (then "continue" after image), "approved"/"build" for code, choose-your-adventure for pages, adjust anytime.

Begin Phase 1 when ready.

Here's the mega prompt (Steal it):

# CONTEXT
You are designing a maximalist, enterprise-grade website for [INSERT YOUR BUSINESS DESCRIPTION]. The goal is to create a visually striking, professionally polished web experience that commands attention while maintaining credibility.

# ROLE
You are an expert web designer and creative technologist specializing in shader-based animations, interactive visual effects, and cutting-edge web experiences. You blend maximalist aesthetics with enterprise-level execution.

---

## Core Requirements
- Project Type: Enterprise-grade website
- Purpose: [INSERT YOUR BUSINESS DESCRIPTION]
- Design Approach: Maximalist aesthetic
- Scope: Minimum 4-5 complete sections

## Website Sections

### Section 1: Hero
- Custom shader-based animated text
- Interactive mouse hover effects
- Beautiful, eye-catching typography
- CRITICAL: Text must be fully responsive and never overflow the viewport

### Section 2-5: Additional Sections
Design at least 3-4 more sections from:
- Services/Features showcase
- About/Company overview
- Portfolio/Case studies
- Testimonials/Social proof
- CTA/Contact section
- Process/How it works

### Visual Treatment (Applied Across All Sections)
- Dithering textures
- Wavy/fluid motion
- Groovy animations
- Rich gradient overlays

## Design Direction

### Style Keywords
maximalist • groovy • wavy • gradients • dithering • enterprise-grade

### Visual Elements to Incorporate
- [ ] Shader effects on hero text
- [ ] Mouse hover-interactive animations
- [ ] Gradient color schemes
- [ ] Dithering texture layers
- [ ] Wavy motion design

## Technical Considerations
- Maintain enterprise-level polish
- Optimize performance despite heavy visuals
- Ensure smooth shader rendering
- Responsive mouse tracking
- CRITICAL: Hero text must use responsive font sizing (clamp, vw units, or media queries)
- CRITICAL: Prevent horizontal overflow - all text must fit within viewport width
- Use proper container constraints and text wrapping
- Test text sizing across mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints

---

## EXECUTION WORKFLOW

### Step 1: Planning & Architecture
- Define website structure and key sections
- Plan shader implementation approach
- Map out user interaction flows
- Outline animation sequences

### Step 2: Design System
- Establish color palette and gradients
- Define typography hierarchy
- Plan dithering and texture treatments
- Design component variations

### Step 3: Technical Implementation
- Set up shader infrastructure
- Implement mouse tracking system
- Build animation framework
- Optimize performance

### Step 4: Final Deliverable
Provide full HTML code for quick preview.
- Complete, functional single-file HTML
- All CSS and JavaScript inline
- Ready to copy/paste and test
- Fully interactive with all effects working

---

text:
Here are real, proven ways people are actually earning a living with LLMs in 2025 (not hype, not “just build an AI wrapper and get rich”). These are the paths where I consistently see people making $3k–$30k+/month once they get traction.

### 1. Specialized Freelance Writing / Ghostwriting (Most accessible, fastest to start)
Most people fail here because they use ChatGPT to write generic blog posts that Google now buries.  
The people making $8k–$25k/month do this instead:

- Target niches where AI still sucks: personal stories, expert interviews, highly technical B2B content (SaaS docs, medical, legal, finance).
- Use Grok/Claude to do 70–80% of the research and outlining, then rewrite everything in your voice with real experiences or client interviews.
- Specialize in one platform/format: LinkedIn ghostwriting for founders, Twitter threads, or long-form newsletters (Substack/Revue).

Real example: Several writers I know charge $1,500–$4,000 per LinkedIn post/thread package for tech executives using Grok to research + Claude to draft + their own editing.

Platforms that still pay well: Upwork (filter for $80+/hr jobs), Contra, and direct outreach to founders on LinkedIn/Twitter.

### 2. Prompt Engineering + Automation Agencies (Highest rates right now)
Companies are desperate for people who can make LLMs actually work reliably.

Two models that work:

A) Solo consultant — $150–$400/hr helping companies build internal AI tools (e.g., customer support automation, sales email sequences, legal contract review).
   → Use Grok 4/Grok + LangChain or custom scripts to prototype solutions in days.

B) Micro-agency — Hire 2–3 juniors in Philippines/India for $5–10/hr to do the grunt work, you handle client comms and final QA. One guy I know went from $0 to $42k/month in 7 months doing this for e-commerce brands (automating product descriptions, ad copy, customer service).

### 3. Selling Custom GPTs / Prompt Packs / AI Tools (Passive-ish income)
The winners aren’t selling “2000 prompts for $29”. They’re doing:

- Building vertical-specific tools and charging $49–$199/month  
  Examples making real money:
  - AI for real estate agents (auto-write listings, respond to leads)
  - AI for lawyers (contract review + red flags)
  - AI for YouTubers (script writing + thumbnail titles + SEO tags)

- Selling access via Gumroad or custom site + Stripe. One creator makes ~$27k/month with an AI tool for Amazon sellers that writes optimized listings.

### 4. Content Flywheel Systems (YouTube/TikTok + Newsletter + Digital Products)
The smartest creators aren’t just posting AI tutorials.

They use this system:
1. Use Grok to research trending topics daily
2. Generate 10–20 short videos per week with AI voice + CapCut
3. Funnel viewers to a $49–$97 mini-course or prompt bundle
4. Use the email list to sell higher-ticket offerings later

One creator using exactly this went from 0 to $18k/month in 4 months posting daily 60-second Grok tips on TikTok.

### 5. AI-Enhanced Services Arbitrage (Dirty but extremely effective)
Offer services that are 90% AI but charge as if they're 100% human.

Examples:
- SEO blog writing ($400–$800 per article, 80% AI-generated + human editing)
- Resume + cover letter packages ($300–$600, using Claude to tailor perfectly)
- Social media management for local businesses ($2k–$5k/month, using AI to generate all posts)

The key: position yourself as an "AI expert" who delivers better/faster results than traditional providers, not as "cheap AI content."

### My personal top 3 recommendations if you're starting today:

1. Pick ONE niche you already know (your old career, hobby, industry) and become the AI expert at using Grok/Claude/GPT-4 in that niche. Authority compounds insanely fast.

2. Start offering one high-ticket service ($1k+) instead of competing on $50 gigs. Even one client per month at $5k beats grinding 50 ChatGPT jobs.

3. Document everything publicly. The people making the most money right now are the ones showing.

text:
Your vibe coded app probably uses Supabase. And if you're trusting Cursor with your DBs, you've probably broken it a few dozen times already. Do this instead:

Before blindly copy and pasting, or even worse letting Cursor change your Supabase via the CLI, feed it this prompt:

"If your changes involve the DB schemas in Supabase, first give me a read-only script that I can copy and paste into Supabase that will clarify any questions or assumptions you are making about the DB schemas. Use UNION to join queries together so the script shows all results in one output, instead of only the last one."

Cursor will then give you a SQL script that you can safely run. Run that script in Supabase → click Export → Copy as JSON → feed back into Cursor. 

Now Cursor has the context it needs to make changes to the DB Schemas without breaking anything. The next change it gives you is now 99% less likely to break shit.
